meta_title: For Students: client workflow to Solve no project ideas (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome no project ideas. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Client Workflow to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Stop staring at a blank IDE. Learn to use a professional client workflow with Antigravity and Claude Code to turn project ideas into portfolio-ready apps.
content.answer_block: The 'no project ideas' paralysis stems from a lack of constraints. By adopting a professional client workflow—defining requirements, scope, and technical stacks before coding—you simulate real-world constraints. Use Antigravity for rapid architecture and Claude Code for iterative execution to transform abstract concepts into production-grade student projects.
content.intro:
The blinking cursor in your IDE shouldn't be a source of anxiety; it should be the threshold of your next breakthrough. Many students get trapped in 'tutorial hell' because they lack the structure of a professional client workflow, leaving them aimless when faced with the blank canvas of a new project. 

When you stop building 'tutorials' and start building 'solutions' for a defined client persona, the code begins to write itself. This guide bridges the gap between academic theory and industry reality, arming you with the precise tooling—Antigravity and Claude Code—to engineer high-impact applications that make recruiters take notice.

steps.title: Define the Client Persona and Problem
steps.description: Don't build 'a todo app.' Build a 'task triage system for freelance graphic designers.' Clarifying the user persona anchors your technical decisions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a senior product manager. Create a detailed project brief for a tool that solves a specific pain point for [User Persona]. Include 3 key features and a success metric.
steps.claude_code_command: claude create --spec 'Design a PRD for a [User Persona] tool with 3 core features focusing on [Pain Point].'

steps.title: Architect the Tech Stack
steps.description: Select tools that demonstrate modern engineering standards. Stick to a stack that you can defend in a technical interview.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Suggest a robust, scalable tech stack for this project. Include database, frontend framework, and AI integration strategy. Explain why this stack is ideal for a portfolio project.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add --tech-stack 'Next.js, Supabase, Tailwind, OpenAI API'

steps.title: Draft the Database Schema
steps.description: A well-defined schema prevents technical debt later. Map out your relationships before writing a single line of application logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a SQL schema or Prisma model for the defined project, including tables for Users, Projects, and AI-generated insights. Ensure strict type safety.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --schema 'Generate Prisma schema for the defined user and project data models.'

steps.title: Implement Core Logic via Iteration
steps.description: Use Claude Code to build features in vertical slices. Focus on the 'happy path' first to get a working prototype quickly.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Break down the core feature into 5 manageable sprint tasks. Provide the logic for the most critical function first.
steps.claude_code_command: claude build --task 'Implement authentication and initial database connection for the core feature.'

steps.title: Refine and Polish for Production
steps.description: Professional apps require error handling and edge-case management. Use your tools to audit your code for security and performance.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review my current codebase for potential security vulnerabilities and performance bottlenecks. Suggest 3 refactors for cleaner, more maintainable code.

pro_tips: Always document your 'Why' in the README; recruiters care more about your decision-making process than the code itself.
Use AI to generate unit tests for your edge cases—it forces you to think about how your app breaks before it actually does.
Limit your scope to one 'killer feature' that solves the primary user pain point, then polish that to perfection.
Treat your GitHub commit history as a professional narrative; write descriptive commits that explain the 'what' and 'why'.
Integrate a logging service early so you can observe how real users (or your testing) actually interact with your system.
content.next_steps: Deploy your project to Vercel or Railway, write a 'Lessons Learned' blog post on Dev.to, and share the link in our community Slack channel for peer review.
content.conversion_block: Stop waiting for the perfect idea and start building the perfect process. Your portfolio is only as strong as your last deployment. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
